title: कैवल्य उपनिषद्
devanagariText:
॥ कैवल्य उपनिषद् ॥

ॐ सह नाववतु। सह नौ भुनक्तु।
सह वीर्यं करवावहै।
तेजस्वि नावधीतमस्तु। मा विद्विषावहै।
ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥

अथाश्वलायनो भगवन्तं परमेष्ठिनमुपसमेत्योवाच —
अधीहि भगवन् ब्रह्मविद्यां वरिष्ठां
सदा सद्भिः सेव्यमानां निगूढाम्।
यया चिराच्सर्वपापं व्यपोह्य
पराक्षरं पुरुषं याति विद्वान्॥ १॥

तस्मै स होवाच पितामहश्च —
श्रद्धाभक्तिध्यानयोगादवैहि॥ २॥

न कर्मणा न प्रजया धनेन
त्यागेनैके अमृतत्वमानशुः।
परेण नाकं निहितं गुहायां
विभ्राजते यद्यतयो विशन्ति॥ ३॥

वेदान्तविज्ञानसुनिश्चितार्थाः
संन्यासयोगाद्यतयः शुद्धसत्त्वाः।
ते ब्रह्मलोकेषु परान्तकाले
परामृतात्परिमुच्यन्ति सर्वे॥ ४॥

विविक्तदेशे च सुखासनस्थः
शुचिः समग्रीवशिरःशरीरः।
अत्याश्रमस्थः सकलेन्द्रियाणि
निरुद्ध्य भक्त्या स्वगुरुं प्रणम्य॥ ५॥

हृत्पुण्डरीकं विरजं विशुद्धं
विचिन्त्य मध्ये विशदं विशोकम्।
अचिन्त्यमव्यक्तमनन्तरूपं
शिवं प्रशान्तममृतं ब्रह्मयोनिम्॥ ६॥

तमादिमध्यान्तविहीनमेकं
विभुं चिदानन्दमरूपमद्भुतम्।
उमासहायं परमेश्वरं प्रभुं
त्रिलोचनं नीलकण्ठं प्रशान्तम्।
ध्यात्वा मुनिर्गच्छति भूतयोनिं
समस्तसाक्षिं तमसः परस्तात्॥ ७॥

स ब्रह्मा स शिवः सेन्द्रः सोऽक्षरः परमः स्वराट्।
स एव विष्णुः स प्राणः स कालोऽग्निः स चन्द्रमाः॥ ८॥

स एव सर्वं यद्भूतं यच्च भव्यं सनातनम्।
ज्ञात्वा तं मृत्युमत्येति नान्यः पन्था विमुक्तये॥ ९॥

सर्वभूतस्थमात्मानं सर्वभूतानि चात्मनि।
सम्पश्यन् ब्रह्म परमं याति नान्येन हेतुना॥ १०॥

आत्मानमरणिं कृत्वा प्रणवं चोत्तरारणिम्।
ज्ञाननिर्मथनाभ्यासात्पापं दहति पण्डितः॥ ११॥

स एवमात्मानमात्मनाऽनुसन्धाय ज्ञानामृतेन तृप्तः।
ज्ञानामृतेन तृप्तः सन्मुच्यते ज्ञानी कैवल्यमाप्नोति।
इत्युपनिषत्॥

ॐ सह नाववतु। सह नौ भुनक्तु।
सह वीर्यं करवावहै।
तेजस्वि नावधीतमस्तु। मा विद्विषावहै।
ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥

॥ इति कैवल्य उपनिषद् सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Kaivalya Upanishad ||

Om Saha nav-avatu | Saha nau bhunaktu |
Saha viryam karavavhai |
Tejasvi nav-adhitam-astu | Ma vidvishavahai |
Om Shantih Shantih Shantih ||

Atha-Ashvalayano bhagavantam Parameshthinam-upasamety-ovacha —
Adhihi bhagavan Brahma-vidyam varishtham
Sada sadbhih sevyamanam nigudhaam |
Yaya chirat-sarva-papam vyapohya
Para-aksharam Purusham yati vidvan || 1 ||

Tasmai sa hovacha Pitamahash-cha —
Shraddha-bhakti-dhyana-yogad-avaihi || 2 ||

Na karmana na prajaya dhanena
Tyagena-ike amritatvam-anaashuh |
Parena nakam nihitam guhayam
Vibhrajate yad-yatayo vishanti || 3 ||

Vedanta-vijnana-sunishchita-arthah
Sannyasa-yogad-yatayah shuddha-sattvah |
Te Brahma-lokeshu para-anta-kale
Para-amritat-parimuchyanti sarve || 4 ||

Vivikta-deshe cha sukha-asana-sthah
Shuchih samagriva-shirah-sharirah |
Aty-ashrama-sthah sakala-indriyani
Nirudhya bhaktya sva-gurum pranamya || 5 ||

Hrit-pundarikam virajam vishuddham
Vichintya madhye vishadam vishokam |
Achintyam-avyaktam-ananta-rupam
Shivam prashantam-amritam Brahma-yonim || 6 ||

Tam-adi-madhya-anta-vihinam-ekam
Vibhum chid-anandam-arupam-adbhutam |
Uma-sahayam Parameshvaram Prabhum
Trilochanam Nilakantham prashantam |
Dhyatva munir-gachchhati bhuta-yonim
Samasta-sakshinam tamasah parastat || 7 ||

Sa Brahma sa Shivah sa-Indrah so-aksharah paramah svarat |
Sa eva Vishnuh sa pranah sa kalo-agnih sa chandramah || 8 ||

Sa eva sarvam yad-bhutam yach-cha bhavyam sanatanam |
Jnatva tam mrityum-atyeti na-anyah pantha vimuktaye || 9 ||

Sarva-bhuta-stham-atmanam sarva-bhutani cha-atmani |
Sampashyan Brahma paramam yati na-anyena hetuna || 10 ||

Atmanam-aranim kritva pranavam cha-uttara-aranim |
Jnana-nirmathana-abhyasat papam dahati panditah || 11 ||

Sa evam-atmanam-atmana-anusandhaya jnana-amritena triptah |
Jnana-amritena triptah san-muchyate jnani Kaivalyam-apnoti |
Ity-Upanishat ||

Om Saha nav-avatu | Saha nau bhunaktu |
Saha viryam karavavhai |
Tejasvi nav-adhitam-astu | Ma vidvishavahai |
Om Shantih Shantih Shantih ||

|| Iti Kaivalya Upanishad Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
अश्वलायन ने भगवान ब्रह्मा (परमेष्ठी) के पास जाकर कहा — भगवन, सर्वश्रेष्ठ ब्रह्मविद्या, जो सत्पुरुषों द्वारा सदा सेवित और निगूढ़ है — जिसके द्वारा विद्वान शीघ्र सब पापों का विनाश कर परमपुरुष को प्राप्त करता है — वह बताइए॥ १॥

उनसे पितामह ने कहा — श्रद्धा, भक्ति, ध्यान और योग से जानो॥ २॥

न कर्म से, न सन्तान से, न धन से — त्याग से ही कुछ लोगों ने अमृतत्व प्राप्त किया। स्वर्ग से परे गुहा में स्थित वह विभ्राजमान है — जिसमें यति (संन्यासी) प्रवेश करते हैं॥ ३॥

वेदान्तविज्ञान में सुनिश्चित, संन्यासयोग से शुद्धसत्त्व — वे परान्तकाल में ब्रह्मलोक में परमामृत से सर्वथा मुक्त होते हैं॥ ४॥

एकान्त स्थान में सुखासन पर बैठ, शुद्ध, सम ग्रीवा-शिर-शरीर, सब इन्द्रियों को रोककर, भक्ति से गुरु को प्रणाम कर — हृत्पुण्डरीक में विरज, विशुद्ध, विशद, विशोक, अचिन्त्य, अव्यक्त, अनन्तरूप, शिव, प्रशान्त, अमृत, ब्रह्मयोनि का चिन्तन करे॥ ५-६॥

उमा-सहाय, परमेश्वर, प्रभु, त्रिलोचन, नीलकण्ठ, प्रशान्त — उनका ध्यान कर मुनि भूतयोनि, समस्तसाक्षी, तमस से परे को प्राप्त होता है॥ ७॥

वही ब्रह्मा, वही शिव, वही इन्द्र, वही अक्षर, परम, स्वराट्। वही विष्णु, प्राण, काल, अग्नि, चन्द्रमा। वही सब कुछ — भूत और भविष्य सनातन। उसे जानकर मृत्यु को पार करता है — मुक्ति का अन्य पथ नहीं॥ ८-९॥

सब भूतों में आत्मा को और आत्मा में सब भूतों को देखता हुआ परम ब्रह्म को प्राप्त होता है — अन्य कोई साधन नहीं॥ १०॥

आत्मा को अधरारणि और प्रणव को उत्तरारणि बनाकर ज्ञान-मन्थन के अभ्यास से पण्डित पाप को दहन करता है॥ ११॥

वह इस प्रकार आत्मा में आत्मा का अनुसन्धान कर ज्ञानामृत से तृप्त होकर मुक्त होता है — ज्ञानी कैवल्य (मोक्ष) प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Complete Upanishad granting Kaivalya — absolute liberation
Na Karmana Na Prajaya — liberation through renunciation alone
Reveals Shiva as Brahma, Vishnu, Indra — the one supreme reality
Teaches meditation on Shiva in the heart-lotus for moksha
Pranava as the churning rod for burning all sins
One of the most concise and powerful Upanishads for seekers
description: Dedicated to Lord Shiva, the Kaivalya Upanishad (कैवल्य उपनिषद्) is a sacred prayer revered in Hindu tradition. This revered text originates from Kaivalya Upanishad (Atharvaveda). Regular recitation is believed to reveal Shiva as Brahma, Vishnu, Indra — the one supreme reality, complete Upanishad granting Kaivalya — absolute liberation, and pranava as the churning rod for burning all sins. This sacred text is ideally recited on Monday (Somvar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Guru Purnima for enhanced blessings. Comprising 11 verses, it can be completed in approximately 10 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Kaivalya Upanishad in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Kaivalya Upanishad in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Complete Upanishad on meditation, Shiva as supreme Brahman, and absolute liberation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Kaivalya Upanishad?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kaivalya Upanishad (कैवल्य उपनिषद्, Kaivalyopanishad) is a short but profound Sanskrit Upanishad belonging to the Krishna Yajurveda recension (also affiliated with the Atharvaveda in some traditions) and counted among the fourteen Shaiva Upanishads. The word kaivalya means absolute aloneness, the supreme state of liberation in which the individual self is realised as identical with the one undivided Brahman. The Upanishad is presented as a dialogue in which the rishi Ashvalayana approaches Brahma the Creator (Pitamaha-Parameshthin) and asks him to teach the supreme Brahma-vidya. Brahma responds that this knowledge is to be attained through shraddha (faith), bhakti (devotion), dhyana (meditation) and yoga, and proceeds to give the meditation on Shiva-as-Brahman in the heart-lotus. The text contains 11 verses preserved in classical Vedic tradition. (Source: Kaivalya Upanishad, Krishna Yajurveda / Atharvaveda recension.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the famous verse Na Karmana Na Prajaya Dhanena?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 3 of the Kaivalya Upanishad — Na karmana na prajaya dhanena, tyagena-eke amritatvam-anashuh — is one of the most quoted moksha-shastra verses in all Vedanta. It declares: Not by ritual action (karma), not by progeny (prajaya), not by wealth (dhana) — by renunciation alone (tyagena) some have attained immortality (amritatvam). The same verse appears in the Mahanarayana Upanishad and the Taittiriya Aranyaka and is recited daily in the Brahma-yajna and Sandhya-vandana of orthodox Brahmins. It is the foundational verse of the sannyasa-dharma — the Hindu doctrine that ultimate liberation is reached not by any worldly attainment (children, wealth, ritual merit) but by complete inner renunciation alone. The very next phrase locates the supreme Self in the cave of the heart (parena nakam nihitam guhayam) — accessible to the disciplined yatis (renunciates). (Source: Kaivalya Upanishad verse 3; Mahanarayana Upanishad.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meditation on Shiva taught in the Kaivalya Upanishad?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 5-7 prescribe the central meditation: in a solitary place (vivikta-deshe), seated comfortably (sukha-asana-stha), with neck, head and body held erect (sama-griva-shirah-sharirah), the indriyas restrained, having bowed to the guru — meditate in the heart-lotus (hrit-pundarikam) on the One who is virajam, vishuddham, vishadam, vishokam (free of dust, pure, lucid, beyond grief), achintyam, avyaktam, ananta-rupam (unthinkable, unmanifest, of infinite forms), Shivam, prashantam, amritam, Brahma-yonim (Auspicious, Peaceful, Immortal, the Source of Brahma). The Upanishad then identifies this Brahman explicitly as Uma-sahayam Parameshvaram Prabhum, Trilochanam Nilakantham — the consort of Uma, the three-eyed Lord with the blue throat — that is, Shiva. The meditator becomes the Witness of all (samasta-sakshi) beyond darkness (tamasah parastat). (Source: Kaivalya Upanishad verses 5-7.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the verse Sa Brahma Sa Shivah Sa Vishnuh teach?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 8 contains one of the supreme statements of Hindu non-dualism: Sa Brahma sa Shivah sa-Indrah so-aksharah paramah svarat, sa eva Vishnuh sa pranah sa kalo-agnih sa chandramah — He alone is Brahma, He is Shiva, He is Indra, He is the supreme Imperishable (akshara) and self-luminous (svarat); He alone is Vishnu, He is the life-breath (prana), He is Time, Fire and the Moon. This verse establishes the Vedantic doctrine that the trimurti (Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva), the devas, the cosmic forces (prana, kala, agni, chandra) and the supreme Imperishable are all one and the same Brahman seen through different upadhis (limiting adjuncts). For Shaiva interpreters this verse demonstrates that Shiva is the supreme Brahman of which all other deities are forms; for Smartas it is one of the foundational vakyas of the pancha-deva worship and Advaita identity. (Source: Kaivalya Upanishad verse 8.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Pranava-arani metaphor of churning the fire of knowledge?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 11 gives a striking ritual metaphor for self-realisation: Atmanam-aranim kritva pranavam cha-uttara-aranim, jnana-nirmathana-abhyasat papam dahati panditah — Making the Self the lower araṇi (fire-stick) and the Pranava (Om) the upper araṇi, the wise person, by the practice of churning through knowledge (jnana-nirmathana-abhyasa), burns up sin (papa). The araṇis are the two ritual fire-sticks rubbed together in the Vedic yajna to produce the sacred fire. The Upanishad transposes this outer ritual into inner sadhana: the meditator churns the lower araṇi (the Atman as object of inquiry) against the upper araṇi (Om, the Pranava, the symbol of Brahman), and through the friction of jnana-abhyasa (sustained practice of knowledge) the fire of wisdom blazes forth, consuming all karma. The verse is one of the principal scriptural authorities for the Pranava-upasana (meditation on Om) tradition. (Source: Kaivalya Upanishad verse 11.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Kaivalya Upanishad be recited and what benefits does tradition assign?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kaivalya Upanishad is most powerfully recited on Monday (Somvar — the day of Shiva), Thursday (Guruvar — the day of the guru and of Vedic study), Maha Shivaratri (the supreme festival of Shiva), Guru Purnima (the festival of the spiritual teacher), and during Pradosha. It is also a standard text for daily svadhyaya in Vedanta gurukulas and ashramas, and is one of the Upanishads chanted at Brahma-yajna. Tradition assigns the following benefits to sincere study and meditation on this Upanishad: (1) the supreme jnana of the identity of Atman and Brahman/Shiva (kaivalya, absolute liberation); (2) burning of all sin (papam dahati panditah) through the churning of the inner fire; (3) freedom from rebirth (na anyah pantha vimuktaye — there is no other path to liberation); (4) realisation of the Self in all beings (sarva-bhuta-stham-atmanam); and (5) the disciple's eligibility for the renunciate life. (Sources: Kaivalya Upanishad phalashruti; classical Vedanta paramparas.)
